title: Bash HereDoc does not invalidate cache
body:
### Issue Description

Consider the following Containerfile

```containerfile
FROM debian:latest

ARG key=value

RUN cat <<EOF > /test.txt
abcd
EOF

RUN sleep 5

# vim: ft=dockerfile
```

Running `podman build -t tmp .` for the first time sleeps for 5 seconds. Running it the second time uses the cache (correct). After changing the text of the RUN command, (e.g. change `abcd` to `efgh`), podman does not invalidate the cache.

This only works if `ARG`  is used _before_ the `RUN` statement.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

comments.body: can you please show the entire build here so we can see exactly what you are seeing ? @flouthoc @nalind can you explain whats going on here ?

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/01f3bc5b-558a-4d42-913c-21aa27feeca0 ---
console log ~/code/podman-demo vi Containerfile ~/code/podman-demo podman build -t asdf . STEP 1/4: FROM debian:latest STEP 2/4: ARG abc=efg --> a29304a815d7 STEP 3/4: RUN cat < /test.txt (1234) --> 001cf4b70d7d STEP 4/4: RUN sleep 2 COMMIT asdf --> d98675457ad4 Successfully tagged localhost/asdf:latest d98675457ad4e48b1d85156db7a5772f1a231cb31b2ef79c73cd4df35fb513da ~/code/podman-demo podman build -t asdf . STEP 1/4: FROM debian:latest STEP 2/4: ARG abc=efg --> Using cache a29304a815d74e574a642689cb3f5d87aaeaf6162a402fd41f0ccbf1de1761be --> a29304a815d7 STEP 3/4: RUN cat < /test.txt (1234) --> Using cache 001cf4b70d7dcb9f7a1a6afc3489271692c63086259944134f78c0dec61c019c --> 001cf4b70d7d STEP 4/4: RUN sleep 2 --> Using cache d98675457ad4e48b1d85156db7a5772f1a231cb31b2ef79c73cd4df35fb513da COMMIT asdf --> d98675457ad4 Successfully tagged localhost/asdf:latest d98675457ad4e48b1d85156db7a5772f1a231cb31b2ef79c73cd4df35fb513da ~/code/podman-demo podman build -t asdf . STEP 1/4: FROM debian:latest STEP 2/4: ARG abc=efg --> Using cache a29304a815d74e574a642689cb3f5d87aaeaf6162a402fd41f0ccbf1de1761be --> a29304a815d7 STEP 3/4: RUN cat < /test.txt (1234) --> Using cache 001cf4b70d7dcb9f7a1a6afc3489271692c63086259944134f78c0dec61c019c --> 001cf4b70d7d STEP 4/4: RUN sleep 2 --> Using cache d98675457ad4e48b1d85156db7a5772f1a231cb31b2ef79c73cd4df35fb513da COMMIT asdf --> d98675457ad4 Successfully tagged localhost/asdf:latest d98675457ad4e48b1d85156db7a5772f1a231cb31b2ef79c73cd4df35fb513da ~/code/podman-demo vi Containerfile ~/code/podman-demo podman build -t asdf . STEP 1/4: FROM debian:latest STEP 2/4: ARG abc=efg --> Using cache a29304a815d74e574a642689cb3f5d87aaeaf6162a402fd41f0ccbf1de1761be --> a29304a815d7 STEP 3/4: RUN cat < /test.txt (1234...) --> Using cache 001cf4b70d7dcb9f7a1a6afc3489271692c63086259944134f78c0dec61c019c --> 001cf4b70d7d STEP 4/4: RUN sleep 2 --> Using cache d98675457ad4e48b1d85156db7a5772f1a231cb31b2ef79c73cd4df35fb513da COMMIT asdf --> d98675457ad4 Successfully tagged localhost/asdf:latest d98675457ad4e48b1d85156db7a5772f1a231cb31b2ef79c73cd4df35fb513da ~/code/podman-demo podman build -t asdf . STEP 1/4: FROM debian:latest STEP 2/4: ARG abc=efg --> Using cache a29304a815d74e574a642689cb3f5d87aaeaf6162a402fd41f0ccbf1de1761be --> a29304a815d7 STEP 3/4: RUN cat < /test.txt (1234...) --> Using cache 001cf4b70d7dcb9f7a1a6afc3489271692c63086259944134f78c0dec61c019c --> 001cf4b70d7d STEP 4/4: RUN sleep 2 --> Using cache d98675457ad4e48b1d85156db7a5772f1a231cb31b2ef79c73cd4df35fb513da COMMIT asdf --> d98675457ad4 Successfully tagged localhost/asdf:latest d98675457ad4e48b1d85156db7a5772f1a231cb31b2ef79c73cd4df35fb513da ~/code/podman-demo echo but does not invalidate cache but does not invalidate cache
This is a bug in buildah because while writing history of heredoc we truncate the actual content, I think we need to include a checksum in the build history instead of truncating it.

I find quite peculiar that this happens only if `ARG` is used before the heredoc. If I use `RUN` or `WORKDIR` instead of `ARG` before the heredoc, the cache is invalidated properly and it works as expected.

https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/0bab4329eebd6d73fa8743e320af873b8dfc5c30/imagebuildah/stage_executor.go#L1947 is returning before the heredocs for the instruction get incorporated into the history text that's being generated and used for comparing to a cache candidate.
